description:
You are the configuration manager for a small fleet of servers.  
    A raw audit log that tracks **every** configuration-file change already exists in  

        /home/user/projects/confmon/logs/config_changes.log  

    Every line in this file has exactly the following pipe-delimited layout (there is a single space before and after every pipe symbol):  

        YYYY-MM-DD hh:mm:ss | USERNAME | ACTION | ABSOLUTE_PATH_TO_FILE | STATUS  

    Example (real line from the log):  

        2022-11-01 09:15:32 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | SUCCESS  

    Your assignment is to produce a filtered, machine-readable report that will later be imported into an inventory system.  
    Follow these functional requirements **exactly**.  The automated test will fail if anything is off, including whitespace.

    1. Filter criteria  
       • USERNAME must be “jdoe”  
       • ACTION must be either “MODIFY” or “DELETE”  
       • The date must be in November 2022 (from 2022-11-01 00:00:00 to 2022-11-30 23:59:59 inclusive).

    2. Output files – create them inside an existing directory:  

           /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/

       a) nov_2022_jdoe_changes.csv  
          • UTF-8 text, newline-delimited.  
          • Header line first, exactly:  

                date,user,action,target,status  

          • Subsequent lines must be the matching log entries converted to comma-separated values in the *same* column order as the header (no extra spaces).  
          • The order of rows **must** preserve the chronological order found in the original log.

          Example of a correctly formatted body row (do **not** hard-code it; generate it):  

              2022-11-20 08:22:18,jdoe,MODIFY,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,SUCCESS  

       b) nov_2022_jdoe_changes.count  
          • Plain text file containing **only** the total number of matching entries, followed by a single trailing newline.  
          • Example if there were 3 matches (not necessarily the real value):  

                3  

    3. Tooling requirements  
       • Use one or more POSIX command-line tools (`grep`, `sed`, `awk`, etc.) that rely on regular expressions to perform the filtering.  
       • You may create temporary files or pipes, but only the two files specified above must remain when you are done.

    4. Verification aid  
       • After finishing, listing the directory  

             ls -l /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/  

         should show exactly two regular files (`nov_2022_jdoe_changes.csv` and `nov_2022_jdoe_changes.count`).  
       • Displaying the count file with  

             cat /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/nov_2022_jdoe_changes.count  

         must output just the integer and a newline.  
       • The `.csv` file’s first line must be the header shown in 2a, and the number of data rows must match the integer written into the `.count` file.

    5. Permissions  
       • Ensure both new files are world-readable (octal 0644) so that future automated jobs can access them without privilege escalation.

    Accomplish the task entirely from the Linux terminal without using any external text editors.
truth:
Pre-existing directories (already present before the agent starts):  
      • /home/user/projects/  
      • /home/user/projects/confmon/  
      • /home/user/projects/confmon/logs/  
      • /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/   ← empty at start

    Pre-existing file and its exact UTF-8 contents (including spaces):  

    Path: /home/user/projects/confmon/logs/config_changes.log  
    Contents (10 lines including a trailing newline at the end of each line):

    2022-11-01 09:15:32 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | SUCCESS
    2022-11-01 10:45:03 | bgates | MODIFY | /etc/ssh/sshd_config | SUCCESS
    2022-11-02 14:11:27 | jdoe | DELETE | /etc/apache2/sites-enabled/000-default.conf | SUCCESS
    2022-10-31 12:00:00 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/passwd | FAILED
    2022-11-15 16:42:05 | root | CREATE | /etc/sysctl.conf | SUCCESS
    2022-11-20 08:22:18 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | SUCCESS
    2022-11-20 08:23:18 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/nginx/mime.types | SUCCESS
    2022-11-30 23:59:59 | jdoe | DELETE | /etc/cron.d/backup | SUCCESS
    2022-12-01 00:00:00 | jdoe | MODIFY | /etc/hosts | SUCCESS
    2022-11-22 11:11:11 | alice | DELETE | /etc/ssh/ssh_config | FAILED

    Desired files after the agent completes the task:

    1) /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/nov_2022_jdoe_changes.csv  
       Mode: 0644  
       Contents (6 lines total, header + 5 data rows, final newline at EOF):

       date,user,action,target,status
       2022-11-01 09:15:32,jdoe,MODIFY,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,SUCCESS
       2022-11-02 14:11:27,jdoe,DELETE,/etc/apache2/sites-enabled/000-default.conf,SUCCESS
       2022-11-20 08:22:18,jdoe,MODIFY,/etc/nginx/nginx.conf,SUCCESS
       2022-11-20 08:23:18,jdoe,MODIFY,/etc/nginx/mime.types,SUCCESS
       2022-11-30 23:59:59,jdoe,DELETE,/etc/cron.d/backup,SUCCESS

    2) /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/nov_2022_jdoe_changes.count  
       Mode: 0644  
       Contents (single line, final newline at EOF):

       5

    The automated test will:  
      • Verify the two files exist with the exact names and permissions.  
      • Confirm the `.csv` file content matches byte-for-byte what is shown above.  
      • Confirm the `.count` file contains exactly “5\n”.  
      • Ensure no extra files are present inside /home/user/projects/confmon/reports/.
